page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-512GB is a memory-optimized virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 64 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7542 processor operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The system features 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This configuration does not include any local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear distinction between single-core and multi-threaded execution. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance falls into the bottom 10%, and single-threaded compression tasks show average speeds. Conversely, multi-threaded capabilities are highly competitive, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression ranking in the top 10% of tested servers. PassMark CPU scores, encryption tests, and physics simulations all place in the top 25%, indicating strong parallel processing efficiency. Memory bandwidth is also a key strength, with small-block read-only and large-block write operations scoring in the top 25%, alongside high-throughput Redis SET performance.

The server's hardware profile presents a clear tradeoff: while single-core execution speeds are modest, the high core count and large memory footprint deliver strong parallel processing and memory throughput. This makes the instance well-suited for memory-intensive, highly threaded workloads such as large-scale database operations, multi-threaded data decompression, and moderate LLM prompt processing. It is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or workloads requiring local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-512GB is a memory-optimized virtual server configured with 64 vCPUs and 64 physical cores on an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio, with no local storage or integrated GPUs. Benchmarks show a distinct tradeoff: single-core performance is limited, ranking in the bottom 10% for stress-ng, but multi-threaded capabilities are robust, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression in the top 10% and PassMark CPU scores in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is strong for small-block reads and large-block writes. This instance is designed for highly parallel, memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, multi-threaded data processing, and caching layers, offering a resource-dense profile without the overhead of dedicated local storage.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-512GB is a memory-optimized KVM server with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 512 GB RAM. It offers strong multi-threaded performance and high memory bandwidth for parallel workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-512GB is an x86_64 KVM server featuring 64 AMD EPYC cores and 512 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized virtual server featuring sixty-four AMD EPYC cores and half a terabyte of RAM for highly parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 64 AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of system memory.
High-capacity 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio optimized for memory-dense tasks.
Strong memory bandwidth and high-throughput Redis SET operations.
No bundled local storage or integrated GPU accelerators.
Best suited for large databases, caching layers, and parallel data processing.
